leagueBriefing: The SEC’s elite remained untouchable on Tuesday as No. 1 Tennessee and No. 6 Alabama both moved to 24-0 with convincing midweek victories. Tennessee tied its best start in school history with a run-rule win over Austin Peay, while Alabama utilized a multi-homer night from Brooke Wells to dispatch Samford. Meanwhile, No. 20 LSU snapped a losing skid with a season-high hit barrage, and No. 25 Duke made a statement on the road by outlasting No. 24 South Carolina in a ranked battle.

games.headline: Lady Vols tie program record with 24-0 start after run-ruling Governors
games.brief: The top-ranked Lady Vols continued their historic tear, matching the 2006 squad for the best start in program history. After Austin Peay managed to tie the game in the fourth, Tennessee's offense shifted into another gear, scoring eight unanswered runs over the final three innings to trigger the mercy rule and keep their perfect season alive.
bullets: Tennessee moves to 24-0, tying the school record for consecutive wins to open a season.
Ella Dodge delivered the game's biggest blow with a bases-clearing three-run double in the fourth.
Makenzie Butt fueled the offense, going yard in the sixth to plate the final two runs.
Sophia Knight recorded her 15th multi-hit game of the season, going 3-for-4 with two runs.
games.context: Tennessee ties the 2006 team for the best start to a season in program history.

games.headline: Wells powers Alabama to 24th straight win with two-homer night
games.brief: No. 6 Alabama showed why they are one of the most dangerous lineups in the country, using a three-homer performance to roll past Samford and maintain their undefeated status. Brooke Wells led the charge with a massive individual effort, while the Crimson Tide pitching staff held the Bulldogs to just three hits in a clinical midweek showing.
bullets: Brooke Wells went 3-for-4 with two home runs and three RBIs, now leading the team with 10 homers.
Alexis Pupillo extended her reached-base streak to 20 games with her ninth home run of the year.
The Crimson Tide pitching staff combined for nine strikeouts, with Vic Moten earning her 10th win.
Alabama has now hit multiple home runs in six of their last seven contests.
games.context: Alabama is now one win away from matching its fourth-best start in program history (25-0 in 2007).

games.headline: Georgia shuts down West Georgia behind multi-hit barrage
games.brief: No. 13 Georgia made quick work of West Georgia, using a 12-hit attack and a combined shutout from Destin Howard and Ada Little to secure a run-rule victory. The Bulldogs' offense was clinical, recording zero strikeouts as a team for the third time this season while scoring in every inning except the first.
bullets: Jaydyn Goodwin led the way with a 3-for-4 night, including the game-sealing RBI single in the fifth.
Freshman Gabi Novickas blasted her second home run of the season, a two-run shot in the fourth.
Destin Howard earned her second win of the year, allowing just three hits over four dominant innings.
The Bulldogs improve to 20-7 overall after a tough opening weekend of SEC play.
games.context: Georgia reached the 20-win mark with the victory.

games.headline: Ammon tosses no-hitter as Bulldogs sweep SEMO in doubleheader
games.brief: Freshman Leila Ammon etched her name into the Bulldogs' record books, throwing a five-inning no-hitter to open a dominant Tuesday sweep. The Bulldogs' bats backed her up immediately, exploding for seven runs in the first inning to effectively end the contest before the sun went down.
bullets: Leila Ammon threw the 27th no-hitter in program history, striking out seven in the process.
The Bulldogs completed the sweep later in the evening with a 4-0 shutout win in the nightcap.
Mississippi State improved to 26-2 overall, marking one of the strongest starts in school history.
SEMO was limited to just four hits across both games of the doubleheader.
games.context: Ammon's no-hitter is the first by a Mississippi State freshman since 2021.

games.headline: LSU snap skid with season-high hit total in rout of Nicholls
games.brief: No. 20 LSU snapped out of an offensive funk in a big way, pounding out a season-high 14 hits to dismantle Nicholls in Thibodaux. After a winless SEC opening weekend, the Tigers' lineup saw every starter record a hit, providing a massive cushion for freshman Cali Deal in her first career start.
bullets: Freshman Cali Deal earned her first collegiate win, allowing only one run on two hits in four innings.
Alix Franklin and Kylee Edwards both set career highs with three hits each.
Avery Hodge broke the game open with her first home run of the season, a three-run blast in the fourth.
LSU extends its winning streak to 38 consecutive games in the all-time series against Nicholls.
games.context: The win ends a three-game losing streak for the Tigers and provides momentum heading into a ranked series with Texas A&M.

games.headline: Virginia explodes for 10 runs in first inning to crush Hampton
games.brief: No. 14 Virginia didn't leave anything to chance on Tuesday, sending 14 batters to the plate in a chaotic 10-run first inning. The early barrage was more than enough for freshman Taylor Smith, who struck out a career-high seven batters in a clinical start that saw the Cavaliers reach their 23rd win of the season.
bullets: Macee Eaton led the first-inning onslaught with an RBI double and a two-run home run in the same frame.
Taylor Smith set a new career high with seven strikeouts over 3.0 innings of one-hit work.
The Cavaliers have now hit 40 home runs as a team in 2026, with Eaton responsible for nine of them.
Virginia moves to 23-2 on the year, continuing their rise into the top 15.
games.context: Virginia's 23-2 start remains one of the best in program history as they prepare for ACC play.

games.headline: Duke silences Gamecocks to secure 100th road win in program history
games.brief: In a ranked midweek battle, No. 25 Duke utilized a balanced 11-hit attack and a complete-game performance from Cassidy Curd to spoil No. 24 South Carolina's night. The victory was a significant milestone for the Blue Devils, marking their 100th true road win in program history and extending their current win streak to seven games.
bullets: Aminah Vega led the offense with a perfect 3-for-3 night, extending her hitting streak to seven games.
Cassidy Curd improved to 9-2 on the year with her third consecutive complete-game effort.
Duke reached the milestone of 100 all-time road wins with the victory in Columbia.
South Carolina's Tori Ensley provided the only Gamecock highlights with a double and a solo home run.
games.context: Duke's seventh straight win helps them climb the rankings after a statement road victory over a top-25 opponent.
